title: Creating dot plots
description: Practice creating dot plots. Dot plots are very similar to frequency tables, but they make it easier to  _see_  the data.
topic_path: Get ready for exploring one-variable quantitative data

assessment_items.question_content:
The following data points represent how many push-ups Mandy did each night before bed this week.

$35$, $32$, $31$, $34$, $35$, $35$, $35$

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a night.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
There were $\blue4$ nights where Mandy did $\pink{35}$ push ups.

[[☃ image 2]]


 
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
The following data points represent how many goals Hank scored each lacrosse season.

$\qquad45,43,45,44,44,44,47$  

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a season.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
There was $\blue1$ season where Hank scored $\pink{43}$ goals.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Kai is a wildlife photographer. The following data points represent the number of his pictures that have been published in each local newspaper.

$\qquad4,6,3,6,5,5$ 

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a local newspaper.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
There was $\blue1$ newspaper where Kai had $\pink{3}$ pictures published.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Hugo competes in fishing tournaments. The following data points represent how many fish he caught in each tournament.

$\qquad27,27,28,28,25,26,27$ 

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a tournament.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
There were $\blue2$ tournaments where Hugo caught $\pink{28}$ fish.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
The following data points represent the number of jars of honey Sandy the Bear consumed each day this week.

$\qquad1,4,2,3,0,3,2$  

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a day.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
There was $\blue1$ day where Sandy the Bear consumed  $\pink{0}$ jars of honey.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Captain Brewster captured a pack of aliens. The following data points represent the lengths of each of the aliens' big toes (in centimeters). 

$\qquad18,16,17,17,16,15,16$  

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a big toe.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
There were $\blue2$ aliens that each have big toes measuring $\pink{17} \text{ cm}$ long.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
The following data points represent the number of losses the Minnesota Igloos have had each season. 

$\qquad10,9,12,10,11,12,11$ 

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a season.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
There were $\blue2$ seasons where the Minnesota Igloos had  $\pink{12}$ losses.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Lola is the coach for the Golden County football team. Below are the number of  wins that the football team had in each of the last six seasons.  

$5,4,7,6,7,5$

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a season.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
There were $\blue2$ seasons where the Golden County football team had $\pink{5}$ wins.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Adam is an opera singer. Below are  the number of times that his voice cracked during each of six concerts.  

$1,0,3,0,2,1$
 
**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a concert.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
There were $\blue2$ concerts where Adam's voice cracked exactly $\pink{1}$ time.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Tanya is a car saleswoman. Below are the number of cars that she sold in each of the last six months.  

$23, 21, 21, 20, 21, 20$

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a month.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
There was $\blue1$ month where Tanya sold $\pink{23}$ cars.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Below are  the number of siblings that each of Sarah's six friends has.  

$4, 1, 0, 3, 2, 2$

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a friend.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
$\blue1$ of Sarah's friends has $\pink{3}$ siblings.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Quarterback Susie has a bad attitude and often gets fined for bad sportsmanship. Below are the costs of each of the six  fines assessed to her this season (in thousands of dollars).   

Cost of fine | Number of fines
:-: | :-: 
$1$ | $1$
$2$ | $4$
$3$ | $1$

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a fine.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
There was $\blue1$ fine that cost $\pink{3}$ thousand dollars.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

labels: Cost of fine in thousands of dollars

assessment_items.question_content:
Lena is training to be a  hairstylist. 
Below are the number of haircuts that she gave during each of her eight training shifts.  

Number of haircuts | Number of shifts
:-: | :-: 
$15$ | $3$
$16$ | $2$
$17$ | $2$
$18$ | $1$

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a training shift.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
There were $\blue3$ training shifts where Lena gave $\pink{15}$ haircuts.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Below are the number of walks that each of Carrie's eight dogs has been on in the last week.  

Number of walks | Number of dogs
:-: | :-: 
$5$ | $1$
$6$ | $2$
$7$ | $3$
$8$ | $1$
$9$ | $1$

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a dog.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
$\blue2$ of Carrie's dogs each went on $\pink{6}$ walks in the last week.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Melanie went on an enchanted walk through the Magical Rain Forest.  The frequency table below shows the number of legs that each of the eight creatures she saw had.  

Number of legs | Number of creatures
:-: | :-: 
$0$ | $2$
$1$ | $1$
$2$ | $3$
$3$ | $2$

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a creature.**

[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
Melanie saw $\blueD2$ creatures that each had $\maroonD{0}$ legs.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
The frequency table below shows how many hours Kim worked each weekend since she started her new job.

Number of hours | Number of weekends
:-: | :-:
$8$ | $1$
$9$ | $3$
$10$ | $2$
$11$ | $1$

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a weekend.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
There were $\blueD2$ weekends where Kim worked $\maroonD{10}$ hours.  

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
The frequency table below shows the number of trick-or-treaters at   Cam's house each year since he started putting pumpkins in his yard.

Number of trick-or-treaters | Number of years
:-: | :-: 
$53$ | $1$
$54$ | $2$
$55$ | $1$
$56$ | $2$


**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a year.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
There were $\blueD2$ years where Cam had $\maroonD{54}$ trick-or-treaters.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Elaine often gets hit by birds when she goes jogging. The frequency table below shows the number of birds that hit her during each jog this week.

Number of birds | Number of jogs
:-: | :-: 
$0$ | $1$
$1$ | $0$
$2$ | $1$
$3$ | $3$
$4$ | $1$

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a jog.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
There was $\blueD1$ jog on which  $\maroonD{0}$ birds hit Elaine.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
The frequency table below shows the resting heart rates (in beats per minute) of each of the eight players on the Michigan Tigers basketball team.  

Resting heart rate (in beats per minute) | Number of players
:-: | :-: 
$80$ | $2$
$81$ | $2$
$82$ | $3$
$83$ | $1$

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a basketball player.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
$\blueD3$ basketball players have a resting heart rate of  $\maroonD{82}$ beats per minute.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

labels: Resting heart rate (in beats per minute)

assessment_items.question_content:
The frequency table below shows how many months old each of Jimmy's nine children was when they began to walk.  

Number of months old | Number of children
:-: | :-: 
$9$ | $2$
$10$ | $3$
$11$ | $2$
$12$ | $2$


**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a child.**
[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
$\blueD3$ of Jimmy's children were $\maroonD{10}$ months old when they began to walk.

[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
The frequency table below shows the number of pets Jamal's classmates own.

Number of pets | Number of classmates
:-: | :-:

$1$ | $4$
$2$ | $3$
$3$ | $0$
$4$ | $1$

**Using this data, create a dot plot where each dot represents a classmate.**

[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
$\blueD3$ of Jamal's classmates own $\maroonD{0}$ pets.
